TargetRLF1: Chlamydia infections are treatable and curable. However, the symptoms are often unnoticeable. If symptoms occur, they may not appear until several weeks after having sex with a partner who has chlamydia. In women, common symptoms of genital infection include: a change in vaginal discharge (white, yellow or grey discharge that may be smelly) , bleeding between menstrual periods or after sex, pain or discomfort in the testicles. If a patient has Lymphogranuloma Venereum (LGV), initial symptoms may include genital itching, burning sensation when urinating, discharge from the penis (mucus-like or clear watery discharge), followed by painful swelling of lymph nodes in the groin. If untreated, it can lead to severe complications such as rectal strictures.

If untreated, it can lead to severe complications such as rectal strictures.
